text.heading: THE XXX UNIVERSITY OF XXX
I The Course Format or Housestyle
1. The Course Guide
text.p: The Course Guide (CG) should precede all other Units of the Course. The CG gives a general overview of the Course and provides useful information to the student about the structure or format of the course; the credit units attached to the course and how they are to be obtained; assessment (assignments and examinations), and the schedule of activities - important dates.
text.heading: 2. The Unit
text.p: A Unit is the smallest segment of an instructional material that can stand alone, but which is also linked to other Units to form a Module. It should have the following characteristics:
i) It should engage an average Distance Learner (DL) student for at least three hours of serious work, which is equivalent to one hour of work under the conventional system.
ii) Depending on the subject matter, a full Unit of structured content should cover between 7 and 10 pages (A4, typeset, 10 or 11 points of Times Roman or any appropriate font)

d) Main content, which should have self-assessment exercises (SAEs) and self-assessment questions (SAQs). The introduction may also contain some SAEs and SAQs (3.0) Particular symbols or icons may be adopted to signal theses activities in the text.
iv) At the end of the Unit, there should be at least 1 standard tutor-marked assignment question (TMAQ), which will form part of the TMAQs al the end of the Module.
v) Tables, diagrams, illustrations should be used where appropriate.
text.heading: 3. The Module
text.p: i) Contains up to 5 Units, which make up a Module. The units, which make up a Module, must be thematically linked to each other.
ii) At the end there should be TMAQs there will be an assignment file, which will contain all the TMAQs at the end of every Unit.
iii) There should be references and a list of recommended books at the end of each Module.
text.heading: 4. The Course
text.p: The Course is made up of all the Modules.
